title: helianthemum hirtum var. teretifolium
intro: Helianthemum hirtum var. teretifolium, also known as Rockrose or Snow Rose, is a perennial plant species within the Cistaceae family. Traditionally, Rockrose has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, offering benefits such as enhanced wound healing, reduced inflammation, and improved immune function. Rockrose is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in folk medicine for centuries, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety in modern medical practices.
